mnemonics.title: M E M O I Z E R
mnemonics.content: M - Memoization saves重复计算; E - Examine subproblems; M - Map states clearly; O - Optimize transition; I - Identify base cases; Z - Zero overlapping work; E - Evaluate from bottom-up if needed.

mnemonics.title: T O P - D O W N
mnemonics.content: T - Think recursively; O - Observe optimal structure; P - Proceed with caching; D - Downward recursion; O - Optimize overlapping; W - Write memoized code; N - Navigate state space.

mnemonics.title: B I T M A S K M A N
mnemonics.content: B - Binary representation; I - Iterate subsets; T - Toggle bits for inclusion/exclusion; M - Mask captures state; A - Analyze each element; S - Store in DP array; K - Keep track of visited.

mnemonics.title: T R E E D P
mnemonics.content: T - Traverse post-order; R - Root the tree; E - Extract subtree info; E - Embed in DP state; D - Define children contributions; P - Propagate upward.

mnemonics.title: D I G I T P O S I T I O N
mnemonics.content: D - Digit by digit processing; I - Identify position constraints; G - Group states by tight/non-tight; I - Index from left to right; T - Tight bound matters; P - Position influences state.

mnemonics.title: C H O P T I M I Z E R
mnemonics.content: C - Convex Hull; H - Hull trick for slopes; O - Optimize transitions; P - Precompute convex; T - Transform linear DP; I - Interpolation for queries; M - Monotonic optimization; I - Iterate optimal points; Z - Zero unnecessary checks.

acronyms.usage: Use when problem has optimal substructure and overlapping subproblems. Choose memoization (top-down) or tabulation (bottom-up) based on state space size.
Find longest subsequence present in both strings. State: dp[i][j] = LCS of first i chars and first j chars.
Find longest increasing subsequence. Use patience sorting O(n log n) or binary search optimization on DP.
Classic bitmask DP: dp[mask][city] = minimum cost visiting all cities in mask ending at city. Complexity O(n² × 2^n).
Represent subsets using binary bits. Each bit indicates inclusion/exclusion of an element. Use for problems like Hamiltonian Path, SET COVER.
Optimize DP transitions of form dp[i] = min(dp[j] + m_j × x_i + b_j). Use when slopes are monotonic.
Optimize DP where dp[i][k] depends on dp[j][k-1] where j < i. Recursively solve halves when opt[j] is monotonic.
Process numbers digit by digit with constraints (non-zero, palindrome, sum of digits). State includes position, tight flag, leading zeros.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine nodes as subproblems and directed edges as transitions. Follow arrows from base states to target state. Count paths = count solutions; find shortest/longest path = find optimal solution.
Visualize 2D DP as a chessboard. Rows and columns are dimensions. Fill cell (i,j) based on neighbors above/left. Diagonal movement for LCS. Trace path backward for reconstruction.
Think of water flowing from leaves to root. Each leaf computes its value and passes upward. Root aggregates all information. Post-order traversal is like bottom-up filling.
Each bit is like a light switch: ON (1) = element included, OFF (0) = excluded. Toggle switches to explore all subsets. Row of n switches = 2^n possible states.
Imagine lines drawn on a board. Query x gives minimum y value among all lines. The lower envelope (convex hull) looks like a piecewise linear bowl. Only lines forming the hull matter.
Each digit position is a door. At each door, choose a digit (0-9). Some doors have locks (tight constraint). Count paths through all doors that satisfy all locks.
Think of a notebook where you solve and record subproblems. Before solving, check if already solved. Recursion tree becomes a DAG when memoized - no redundant work.
Diagonal fill from small intervals to large. dp[i][j] = optimal for substring i to j. Expand outward from each base case. Final answer at top-right corner.

analogies.analogy: Calculating Fibonacci numbers without memoization is like asking the same person for directions repeatedly instead of writing them down. With memoization, it's like checking your notes first.
Finding shortest path in a graph is like breaking a long journey into shortest segments between cities. If each segment is optimal, the whole journey is optimal.
Planning a party guest list where each person has conflicts with others. Each guest is a light switch - ON if invited. Try all combinations by flipping switches to find valid group.
Ancestor information in family tree. Each person inherits traits from children. Computing from youngest generation upward gives complete family history at root.
Password cracking with constraints. Each digit position is a lock tumbler. Count valid combinations by trying each digit at each position while respecting rules.
Choosing best shipping method among multiple carriers. Each carrier has different base cost and per-kg rate. For any package weight, find minimum cost by checking only 'dominant' carriers.
Organizing a library where books have dependencies. Instead of arranging all books, only track which chapters of each subject are placed. Reduces huge permutations to manageable states.
Following a treasure map backwards. You know the final treasure location (answer). Trace decisions step-by-step from end to start to reconstruct the complete solution path.
